dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 5988 IH: Ceasing Outrageous Restitution and Reimbursement Under President Trump Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Watson Coleman (for herself, Ms. Norton, Mr. Fields, Mr. Moulton, Mr. Jackson of Illinois, Ms. Craig, Mrs. McIver, Mr. Carson, Ms. Tlaib, Ms. Clarke of New York, Mr. Green of Texas, and Ms. Lee of Pennsylvania) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend section 2680 of title 18, United States Code, to provide for an exception to the Federal Tort Claims Act.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Ceasing Outrageous Restitution and Reimbursement Under President Trump Act or the CORRUPT Act.

section.header: Exception to Federal tort claims act
section.text: Section 2680 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: Any claim brought by the President of the United States or a covered relative of such President during the term for which such President shall have been elected. For the purposes of this subsection, a covered relative means a spouse, child, sibling, or in-law of the President.
